Incident Report – Cyber/Data Access Event (Lion City Exchange Main List issuer: Alto Meridian Holdings Ltd.), Singapore, 14 May 2026 22:15 SGT; context: ongoing M&A due diligence “Project Kestrel” and capital-markets blackout under the Securities and Futures Act.
Mr. Daniel Teo Wei Jian (b. 1987-05-03, approx. 39), contract systems analyst seconded from NexPillar Services Pte Ltd to Alto Meridian, accessed internal share “/CorpFin/Capital Projects” from AMH Bugis site during change window.
Cluster identifiers: AD user amhl\dteowj, mailbox daniel.teo@nexpillar.example, Le n o v o T14 asset ALM-T14-0 5 9, temp VPN profile vp n-contr-117, one-time session “SE SS-9a3d4f1” (see https://sec.alto-meridian.example/audit?sid=SESS-9a3d4f1&user=amhl%5Cdteowj), HR contractor ID C0837.
Content touched included a draft term sheet for Project Kestrel and a preliminary employee equity roll; if generally disclosed, such information could reasonably be expected to affect price/valuation (MNPI risk under SFA), although the download log shows only metadata views and a 16 kB thumbnail.
Based on current facts, the event does not constitute a material adverse change (MAC) for the issuer and did not involve any external transmission; no trading by insiders has been detected during the blackout.
PDPA assessment: business contact information (names, corporate emails, job titles) of approx. seven employees was visible; no NRIC, passport, biometric, or home addresses were collected or exported; “do not share passwords” policy remains in force and was re-communicated.
Employment/M&A workflow: contractor NDA and vendor DPA in place; firmwide retention schedule applied; HR has logged a coaching note but no disciplinary action has been taken pending final forensics.
Listing obligations (LCE Listing Manual r.703) and SFA s.218 considerations reviewed; preliminary view is that no immediate announcement is required, subject to Board oversight and the absence of further indicators of compromise.
Separate weak identifiers not linked to any named person: DOB 02/1991, Tampines commuter, Android 12 build SP1A.210812.016, device serial NX-9G4K, card suffix **73 29, wifi SSID “office-guest”, none of which is individually identifying.
Note: a printed log line shows “ac c t: ** 7 3 2 9 / loc: TP Hub / dev: NX- 9 G 4 K”, which appears as OCR artefacts from a scanned ticket; mac: n/a (this is not a MAC address string).
Operational remediation completed: reset amhl\dteowj credentials, disabled vp n-contr-117, tightened share permissions, rotated vendor OTP seed, and deployed SentriGuard v5.2 EDR policy AMH-EDR-Blue to contractor devices.
Public-source cross‑check: Q3 investor slides (public) already reference a “pipeline platform expansion” without pricing; this report does not add to the public narrative and contains no undisclosed revenue guidance.
lowercase pm notes: triage complete, owners assigned, backlog item for dlp tuning, no blockers; next update to audit committee draft by fri eod.
For legal queries: Ms. Leena Chia, Partner, Fern & Fox Law LLC (Singapore solicitors), leena.chia@fernfoxlaw.example, +65 6998 1234.
Issuer contact: security@alto-meridian.example; incident ticket AM-INC-2026-0514; please quote “SE SS-9a3d4f1” in correspondence.
